title: 10 medicinal constituents of Cananga odorata (ylang-ylang)

constituents_list.constituent_desc: Cananga odorata linalool is a naturally occurring compound found in the essential oil of the plant. It is responsible for its sweet and floral fragrance. Linalool is a sesquiterpene that has been traditionally used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation, reduce stress and anxiety, and improve mood. Its calming properties make it an effective ingredient in skincare products, helping to soothe and calm irritated skin.

Cananga odorata benzyl benzoate is a naturally occurring ester extracted from the flowers of the Cananga tree. It is often used in aromatherapy and perfumery due to its sweet, floral, and slightly woody scent. Benzyl benzoate is also known for its insecticidal properties, making it effective against mosquitoes and other pests. In addition, it has been shown to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it a popular ingredient in skincare products.

Cananga odorata geraniol is a natural essential oil extracted from the flowers of this tropical plant. Geraniol is a sweet and floral oil that has been used for centuries in aromatherapy, perfumery, and traditional medicine. It is known for its calming and relaxing properties, and is often used to reduce stress and anxiety. Geraniol also possesses antiseptic and anti-inflammatory qualities, making it beneficial for skin care and wound healing.

Cananga odorata cinnamaldehyde is a natural compound extracted from the flowers of the Cananga odorata tree. It is a yellowish oil with a sweet and slightly spicy aroma, often used in perfumery, aromatherapy, and traditional medicine. Cinnamaldehyde has been found to have various benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and sedative properties, making it a popular ingredient in skincare products, fragrances, and herbal remedies.

Cananga odorata α-pinene is a sesquiterpene compound that is found in the essential oil of the plant. It is one of the most abundant components of ylang-ylang oil and is responsible for its sweet, floral aroma. α-Pinene has been shown to have various physiological effects, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.

Cananga odorata β-phellandrene is a sesquiterpene compound found in the essential oil of this fragrant flower. It is responsible for the sweet, floral, and slightly spicy aroma of ylang-ylang oil, which is widely used in perfumery, aromatherapy, and traditional medicine. β-Phellandrene has been shown to have several health benefits, including reducing stress, anxiety, and blood pressure, as well as having antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties.

Cananga odorata linalyl acetate is a natural ester extracted from the flowers of the Cananga tree. It is a key component in essential oils and is prized for its sweet, floral, and slightly spicy aroma. Linalyl acetate has been used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and alleviate anxiety, while also exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal properties that make it useful in skincare products.

Cananga odorata nerolidol is a sesquiterpene compound found in the essential oil of this fragrant flower. It is a major component of ylang-ylang oil, accounting for about 40% of its composition. Nerolidol has been studied for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, as well as its ability to reduce stress and anxiety. Its sweet, floral scent is also used in aromatherapy and perfumery applications.

Cananga odorata cineole is a natural essential oil extracted from the flowers of the Cananga tree. It is a primary constituent of ylang-ylang oil and responsible for its distinct sweet, floral, and slightly woody aroma. Cineole has various therapeutic benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it widely used in aromatherapy, perfumery, and traditional medicine. Its unique scent also makes it a popular ingredient in cosmetics and fragrances.

Cananga odorata geranyl acetate is a natural compound extracted from the flowers of the Cananga tree. It is a key constituent of the essential oil of ylang-ylang and is responsible for its distinctive sweet, floral, and slightly spicy fragrance. Geranyl acetate has been traditionally used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve mood. Its scent is often used in perfumes, skincare products, and fragrances due to its intense and lingering aroma.

intro: Cananga odorata has active constituents such as volatile oils, flavonoids, and sesquiterpenes. These compounds confer medicinal properties that include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic effects. The constituents of ylang-ylang oil can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce stress and anxiety, and improve cardiovascular health by lowering blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties make it effective against infections and wounds, allowing for faster healing and reduced risk of complications. These properties can significantly improve the quality of life for individuals suffering from various ailments, promoting overall well-being and enhancing their mental and physical health.
intro_desc: Cananga odorata has active constituents such as linalool, linalyl acetate, and benzyl acetate. These constituents are responsible for its sedative, anti-anxiety, and anti-inflammatory properties. Ylang-ylang has been traditionally used to reduce stress and anxiety, promote relaxation, and soothe skin irritations. Its constituents can improve people's lives by providing a natural remedy for insomnia, anxiety disorders, and skin conditions, promoting emotional and physical well-being.
